model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Ohio State enters as the slight 2.5-point betting favorite and holds a meaningful edge in overall résumé quality, ranking #29 in the NET compared to TCU's KenPom #43, with a 21-12 record forged against one of the toughest schedules in the country in the Big Ten. The Buckeyes demonstrated late-season resilience with wins over Purdue and Iowa in the final stretch and carry Big Ten battle-testing that translates well to tournament play. TCU's case is genuinely credible — their defense ranks in the top 30 nationally (KenPom AdjD: 97.8) and Jamie Dixon's Horned Frogs are capable of grinding out a low-possession upset — but Ohio State's superior NET ranking, schedule strength, and the market's slight lean make them the right side in this classic 8/9 coin-flip game.

reasoningStep.title: Ohio State vs. TCU — East 8/9 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Ohio State (8-seed, NET #29, 21-12, Big Ten) is a slight 2.5-point favorite over TCU (9-seed, KenPom #43, 22-11, Big 12). TCU's elite defense (top-30 nationally by Torvik AdjDE) gives them a credible upset path, but Ohio State's superior NET ranking, Big Ten schedule strength, and market lean tip the pick to the Buckeyes in a genuinely close game.
evidence: Ohio State NET #29, record 21-12 (12-8 Big Ten), Quad 1 record 5-11 with wins over Northwestern, UCLA, Wisconsin, Purdue, and Iowa — strong résumé built against elite competition.
TCU KenPom #43, AdjEM +17.62, AdjO 115.5 (81st nationally), AdjD 97.8; Torvik #48, AdjDE 99.63 (30th nationally) — elite defense is TCU's primary weapon.
Ohio State opened as a 2.5-point favorite over TCU with a total of 147.5, reflecting the market's slight lean toward the Buckeyes.

No significant injury concerns surfaced for either team heading into the March 19 tip in Greenville, South Carolina; TCU lost to Kansas in Big 12 Tournament quarterfinals, Ohio State lost to Michigan in Big Ten Tournament quarterfinals.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/basketball/2026/03/15/ohio-state-tcu-march-madness-bracket-ncaa-tournament-2026/89025398007/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Ohio State March Madness opponent is TCU in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Ohio State basketball’s March Madness opponent is TCU to open 2026 NCAA Tournament bracket Adam Jardy Columbus Dispatch For the first time in four years, the Ohio State men's basketball team is returning to the NCAA Tournament. After compiling a 21-12 record, earning a No. 8 seed in the Big Ten Tournament and battling Michigan to a four-point loss in the quarterfinals, the Buckeyes are a No. 8 seed in the 2026 men's NCAA Tournament. They will open March Madness against No. 9 seed TCU in Greenville, South Carolina, at 12:15 p.m. March 19. The Horned Frogs, coached by Jamie Dixon, went 22-11 overall and 11-7 in the Big 12 Conference. TCU closed the regular season by winning eight of its final nine games. With a win, the Buckeyes would likely face overall No. 1 seed Duke in the second round. Only three of Ohio State's 15 players have been to an NCAA Tournament and only two of them have played in one. Puff Johnson played in six games at North Carolina and watched a seventh while injured, Josh Ojianwuna was part of six March Madness games at Baylor and played in four while Brandon Noel redshirted and watched two Wright State NCAA Tournament games. Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today! Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! The Buckeyes climbed their way into the NCAA Tournament thanks to a late-season winning streak. Ohio State was 17-11 overall and 9-8 in the Big Ten after a 17-point road loss to Iowa on Feb. 25 and facing a game against No. 8 Purdue on March 1. It won that game 82-74 and followed that with a 94-62 win at Penn State, a 91-78 win against Indiana on senior day and a 72-69 win against Iowa in the Big Ten Tournament. By that point, the Buckeyes had played themselves off the bubble and firmly into position for an at-large bid. For senior guard Bruce Thornton , what will be his 136th game will also be his first in March Madness. His 135 games without an NCAA Tournament appearance are the most in Ohio State history. Buy Ohio State NCAA Tournament tickets The Buckeyes will be seeking their first Sweet 16 appearance since the 2012-13 season. They have won at least one game in eight of their last 10 NCAA Tournament appearances. Ohio State men's basketball beat writer Adam Jardy can be reached at ajardy@dispatch.com, on Bluesky at @ cdadamjardy.bsky.social or on Twitter at @ AdamJardy . Facebook Twitter

This is helpful to determine the chances of teams being selected for the NCAA Tournament during March Madness. Some example use cases would be: to visualize how close a Q2 opponent is to becoming a higher quality Q1 opponent; to determine whether one team has more quality wins than another team; to check how many opportunities for quality wins remain on a team's schedule. This app is not associated with the NCAA or any institutions represented on this website. All marks and logos are trademarks of their respective owners. A green up arrow means that the team's NET rank is trending upwards compared to the previous rank, while a red down arrow means that the team's NET rank is trending downwards. A gray neutral icon means that the ranking has not changed. The previous rank can be seen by opening the team's page. An opponent's current NET ranking is displayed in parentheses next to the trending icon Ohio St. is in the Big Ten conference and has a 5-11 Quad I record, a 4-1 Quad II record, a 6-0 Quad III record, and a 6-0 Quad IV record. You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.text: Ohio State March Madness opponent is TCU in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Ohio State basketball’s March Madness opponent is TCU to open 2026 NCAA Tournament bracket Adam Jardy Columbus Dispatch For the first time in four years, the Ohio State men's basketball team is returning to the NCAA Tournament. After compiling a 21-12 record, earning a No. 8 seed in the Big Ten Tournament and battling Michigan to a four-point loss in the quarterfinals, the Buckeyes are a No. 8 seed in the 2026 men's NCAA Tournament. They will open March Madness against No. 9 seed TCU in Greenville, South Carolina, at 12:15 p.m. March 19. The Horned Frogs, coached by Jamie Dixon, went 22-11 overall and 11-7 in the Big 12 Conference. TCU closed the regular season by winning eight of its final nine games. With a win, the Buckeyes would likely face overall No. 1 seed Duke in the second round. Only three of Ohio State's 15 players have been to an NCAA Tournament and only two of them have played in one. Puff Johnson played in six games at North Carolina and watched a seventh while injured, Josh Ojianwuna was part of six March Madness games at Baylor and played in four while Brandon Noel redshirted and watched two Wright State NCAA Tournament games. Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today! Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! The Buckeyes climbed their way into the NCAA Tournament thanks to a late-season winning streak. Ohio State was 17-11 overall and 9-8 in the Big Ten after a 17-point road loss to Iowa on Feb. 25 and facing a game against No. 8 Purdue on March 1. It won that game 82-74 and followed that with a 94-62 win at Penn State, a 91-78 win against Indiana on senior day and a 72-69 win against Iowa in the Big Ten Tournament. By that point, the Buckeyes had played themselves off the bubble and firmly into position for an at-large bid. For senior guard Bruce Thornton , what will be his 136th game will also be his first in March Madness. His 135 games without an NCAA Tournament appearance are the most in Ohio State history. Buy Ohio State NCAA Tournament tickets The Buckeyes will be seeking their first Sweet 16 appearance since the 2012-13 season. They have won at least one game in eight of their last 10 NCAA Tournament appearances. Ohio State men's basketball beat writer Adam Jardy can be reached at ajardy@dispatch.com, on Bluesky at @ cdadamjardy.bsky.social or on Twitter at @ AdamJardy . Facebook Twitter
